title: APT Design Lint Gates
source: Extracted from applied-practical-thinking design-system lint checklist
merge_policy: Critical failures block promotion unless an explicit decision record accepts the risk.
critical_gates: Brand identity is visible and consistent with the project profile.
Color usage is token-based and accessible.
Navigation, action, status, selection, focus, hover, and disabled states use the semantic color roles from design-tokens.json.
Primary calls to action, links, focus rings, and high-frequency action emphasis use the blue primary/action role.
The restricted accent is not the default hover, focus, CTA, or active navigation color; use it only for explicit support semantics such as badges, callouts, charts, and success treatment.
Raw color values, teal-as-default-CTA treatment, missing focus color, or ambiguous active navigation are critical failures unless a decision record accepts the exception.
Danger, warning, and success colors are used for semantic feedback only, not decoration.
Typography scale fits the surface and does not overflow containers.
Spacing and layout use stable responsive constraints.
Cards are used only for individual framed objects, not nested page structure.
Components use shared patterns before inventing new interaction shapes.
Motion is subtle, short, and non-blocking.
Loading, empty, success, error, disabled, and permission states are defined where applicable.
Content is clear, specific, and aligned to APT naming and messaging rules.
Keyboard, focus, contrast, and reduced-motion expectations are met.
AI-generated UI does not introduce unsupported styles or hidden behavior.
evidence_required: Token/component notes covering navigation, actions, status, selection, focus, hover, and disabled color roles
Accessibility and contrast notes for primary, secondary, disabled, danger, warning, and success states
